title: CDI doesn't load from /run/cdi/ by default as before
body:
### Issue Description

Podman 5.5.1 doesn't read from /run/cdi/ as before.
I can confirm that Podman 5.4.0 was reading from /run/cdi/ (or maybe from /var/run/cdi/ which is a symlink since long ago).

So this change can break some working setups from people using /run/cdi/ to generate files in there on boot instead of using /etc/cdi/ which isn't so much appropriate for dynamic things (if you are on cloud and change GPUs on the same server between restarts for example).

### Steps to reproduce the issue

```bash
nvidia-ctk cdi generate --output=/run/cdi/nvidia.yaml
podman run --rm --device nvidia.com/gpu=all --security-opt=label=disable \
    nvcr.io/nvidia/cuda:12.8.1-runtime-rockylinux9 nvidia-smi
```

### Describe the results you received

Error: setting up CDI devices: unresolvable CDI devices nvidia.com/gpu=all

### Describe the results you expected

Correct output from nvidia-smi

comments.body:
I think this could be a consequence of dce36131ae7ce0211cf8222ab84c63c7f0ea78de.

Maybe we should add `DefaultDynamicDir = /var/run/cdi` to the list of `cdi_spec_dirs` in common.

Probably you've already seen it, but in the meanwhile as a workaround you can specify `cdi_spec_dirs` in the `/etc/containers/containers.conf` file or specify or `--cdi-spec-dir` on the command line

> Maybe we should add DefaultDynamicDir = /var/run/cdi to the list of cdi_spec_dirs in common.

That is already done in https://github.com/containers/common/pull/2462, just need a second review to merge it

Thanks all!!!
This is actually a duplicate of https://github.com/containers/common/issues/2460 and the PR has been merged so all good, next release should avoid this issue for people that haven't upgraded yet.
Hope it gets released and available on repos soon.
